topics: FFmpeg and VLC open source media infrastructure
Video codec compression and decoding pipeline
Container formats vs. codec distinctions
VLC's fault-tolerant design philosophy
Open source engineering culture and community

claims.claim: FFmpeg underlies the video processing pipelines of YouTube, Netflix, Chrome, Firefox, VLC, Discord, and the vast majority of video platforms, with over 90% of video processing workflows estimated to involve it.
evidence: Named platforms: YouTube, Netflix, Chrome, Firefox, VLC, Discord, OBS
'It is estimated that over 90% of video processing workflows online and offline involve FFmpeg'
'You're on YouTube. Well, it's FFmpeg on the client side. Well, on the server side, the client side is probably Chrome. Well, you're using FFmpeg also.'

claims.claim: VLC has been downloaded over 6.5 billion times, and the true number is likely higher because downloads cannot be fully counted.
evidence: 'VLC has been downloaded over six billion times'
'VLC has been downloaded at least 6.5 billion times. But likely that number, because it's impossible to really count the number, is much higher than that.'

claims.claim: Video compression ratios of 100x to 1000x are achieved by exploiting spatial and temporal redundancy and modeling human perceptual limitations, not by lossless methods like ZIP.
evidence: 'When you move to video, you need 100 times, 200 times'
'The target is 1000x'
'Compression is not like a zip... Here, we are degrading the signal'
'We move to YUV, which is basically one is luminance, brightness, and the other are colors. And this matches your eyes'

claims.claim: Each new generation of video codec achieves approximately 30% better compression at the same quality, but requires orders of magnitude more computational power to encode.
evidence: 'Each generation of the codec is like 30% less for the same quality'
'It's 30% better, but an order of magnitude, perhaps even two orders of magnitude, more compression power'

claims.claim: VLC's resilience to broken and malformed files stems from its origin as a client for UDP-based streaming, where packet loss is expected, instilling a 'don't trust your inputs' philosophy from the start.
evidence: 'VLC is just a client of a streaming solution called VideoLAN from a very long time ago, from the late 90s'
'When you're playing video, which are on UDP, right, in network, they might be damaged'
'VLC was just like, hey, this file is broken, but I'm still going to try to interpret it. And this was very useful' (regarding pirated AVI files with metadata at end)

claims.claim: VLC and FFmpeg probe file contents rather than trusting file extensions, because real-world files frequently have incorrect or misleading extensions.
evidence: 'In VLC and FFmpeg, we discard the file format, right? We look into the file to understand what's in it'
'We analyze in real time everything that we have, and we don't trust the format'
'The file may say .mp4, but it may be something completely different'

claims.claim: Up to 45% of video files are not GPU-decodable and require software fallback decoding in VLC.
evidence: 'I think it's up to 45% of files are not GPU decodable. So these need to be probed. They need to be detected.'

claims.claim: Modern codecs like AV1 and VVC are not single codecs but collections of tools tailored to different content types such as screen sharing, animation, and live video.
evidence: 'Modern codecs like AV1, AV2 or VVC are actually not codecs, they are a collection of tools'
'You may be on Zoom and sharing your PowerPoint, and then you need to show the audience a video. That codec needs to start changing its tool set depending on the content'
AV1, AV2, VVC named explicitly

quote_candidates.text: Compression is not like a zip, right? A zip, you have data in, you get data out, right? And you try with all the zip compression to arrive with the limit. Here, we are degrading the signal, right? And so we need to degrade both the audio and the video signal in the best way possible.
quote_candidates.speaker: Jean-Baptiste Kempf

quote_candidates.text: Everything in VLC is prepared to work with broken files. And it's a philosophical idea from the beginning, and everything is engineered into that. And it's a culture.
quote_candidates.speaker: Jean-Baptiste Kempf

quote_candidates.text: The real world is a completely different place to a three-letter file format.
quote_candidates.speaker: Kieran Cunha

quote_candidates.text: It wouldn't be a surprise, you know, these big companies just have 3,000-line FFmpeg commands.
quote_candidates.speaker: Kieran Cunha

quote_candidates.text: Every sentence is someone's lifetime's work. There are books about every sentence. So the level of complexity in many cases is inordinate.
quote_candidates.speaker: Kieran Cunha

quote_candidates.text: From a philosophical level, I think it's incredible that your home videos, your grandmother's home videos and trillion-dollar corporations effectively are on a level playing field using the same technologies.
quote_candidates.speaker: Lex Fridman

guest_candidates.name: Kieran Cunha
guest_candidates.title: FFmpeg Contributor; FFmpeg Twitter Account Operator

topics: Open source licensing and community governance
VLC and VideoLAN origin story
FFmpeg volunteer developer culture and code quality standards
Google AI-generated security reports controversy
Monetization decisions and ethical trade-offs in open source

claims.claim: FFmpeg and VLC democratized video technology that previously required hundreds of thousands of dollars of equipment the size of a car, enabling the podcast and YouTube revolution.
evidence: In the 90s, compression equipment cost hundreds of thousands of dollars and was the size of a car
FFmpeg described as 'a big player' in the podcast and streaming revolution
Tools like FFmpeg created 'almost an exact level playing field'

claims.claim: Relicensing an open source project with thousands of contributors is practically and emotionally extremely difficult because copyright is held by all individual contributors.
evidence: JB had to contact more than 350 people to relicense VLC's core from GPL to LGPL
JB physically traveled to find contributors, including visiting the father of a deceased contributor
Some contributors whose code was deleted still hold copyright because subsequent work was based on theirs

claims.claim: VLC originated from a 1990s student network project at École Centrale Paris, not from a single founder's creation.
evidence: Network 2000 project built to stream MPEG-2 satellite video over an ATM network at 155 Mbps
VideoLAN project started by students including Christophe Mathieu around 1995-1996
VLC made open source in early 2001; JB joined only in 2003

claims.claim: Jean-Baptiste Kempf repeatedly refused tens of millions of dollars in offers to bundle spyware, toolbars, or ads into VLC to keep it free and ethical.
evidence: Offers included bundling toolbars described as 'basically spyware' and changing search engines
JB states: 'The last offer I had was obscene'
JB says accepting would have destroyed the project within three years through community forking

claims.claim: Google used AI to generate high-volume security bug reports on FFmpeg, including on obscure 1990s game codecs, and went to media before fixes could be made, creating a de facto denial-of-service on volunteer maintainers.
evidence: Reports described as 'very wordy' and 'almost a denial of service by AI generated bug reports'
Google provided a standard 90-day industry deadline without accounting for volunteer-driven development
Vulnerability was on 'an obscure 1990s game codec'
Google announced AI capabilities to media before issues could be fixed
speakers: Kieran (speaker referenced but not fully named)

claims.claim: The core maintenance teams of VLC and FFmpeg are tiny relative to their contributor bases, requiring very high code quality standards to remain maintainable.
evidence: Core community of VLC is five people; core community of FFmpeg is 10 to 15
Only ~1% of contributors stay long-term
Thousands of contributors over the lifetime but only a handful maintain continuously

claims.claim: Linus Torvalds' creation of Git is argued to be more significant than his creation of the Linux kernel.
evidence: Linux powers ~70-80% of Microsoft Azure servers and all Android phones
Speaker states 'what he did on Git is more interesting than what he did on the Linux kernel'

quote_candidates.text: In open source, we make, we give you everything. And that managed to get a lot of people work together.
quote_candidates.speaker: Jean-Baptiste Kempf

quote_candidates.text: The license is a social contract in terms of Rousseau de facto of the community. The community does not agree on much besides the license.
quote_candidates.speaker: Jean-Baptiste Kempf

quote_candidates.text: I do not feel that I'm completely legitimate to do that.
quote_candidates.speaker: Jean-Baptiste Kempf

quote_candidates.text: I arrived to the work of a person who was a factory worker. And I said, well, I need you to sign that because it was his son who died, who actually wrote the code.
quote_candidates.speaker: Jean-Baptiste Kempf

quote_candidates.text: We care about excellent code. We don't care who you are. Maybe you're a dog. I don't care.
quote_candidates.speaker: Jean-Baptiste Kempf

quote_candidates.text: The last offer I had was obscene and they say, yeah, but imagine with all that money, you could build something new. Open source, right? It was like the mind trick was it was difficult.
quote_candidates.speaker: Jean-Baptiste Kempf

guest_candidates.name: Kieran
guest_candidates.title: FFmpeg developer/contributor (surname not given in chunk)

topics: Open source funding and security researcher incentives
FFmpeg/VLC binary star system and ecosystem interdependencies
Reverse engineering proprietary video codecs
Key historical contributors and eras of FFmpeg
Open source community motivation and contribution culture

claims.claim: Google uses FFmpeg at a scale of millions of CPU cores but contributes disproportionately little outside its own product areas like VP9 and AV1
evidence: Google funds students and Google Summer of Code but contributions are mostly regarding their own products
After a debacle Google started sending patches and introduced reward tools for fixing issues

claims.claim: Security researchers are incentivized to hype vulnerabilities while open source maintainers receive no recognition for fixing them, creating misaligned incentives
evidence: Alex Strange's Hacker News post: 'security people are rampant self-promoters'—Google gives million dollar bounties, Defcon prizes, while fixers get nothing
A pixel wrong-color integer overflow was marked high 7.5 severity in red
A 16-year-old contributor (Rukai Peng) found and fixed issues without creating a CVE drama, fixing within three days

claims.claim: Microsoft Teams posted on the FFmpeg bug tracker treating it like a vendor SLA, then offered only a one-time payment of a few thousand dollars when asked for a support contract
evidence: FFmpeg tweet: 'trillion dollar corporations expect free and urgent support from volunteers'
Microsoft Teams name-dropped their product as high priority on the public bug tracker
After requesting a long-term support contract, Microsoft offered a one-time payment of a few thousand dollars

claims.claim: VLC is to FFmpeg as Android is to Linux—they are a binary star system that co-exist and succeed because of each other
evidence: Over 80% of FFmpeg pipelines depend on x264, a VideoLAN project
VLC uses FFmpeg, gives it reach and exposure to weird files, and historically used donation money to fund FFmpeg development
Many developers are shared between FFmpeg and VLC

claims.claim: FFmpeg is likely running on 100 million to a billion CPUs simultaneously, making every instruction optimization have massive real-world impact
evidence: JB: 'we should expand ffmpeg is probably one of the biggest cpu users in the world'
'easily 100 order of magnitude 100 million maybe even a billion cpus as we speak'

claims.claim: Reverse engineering a 20-30 megabyte binary blob, as Kostya Shishkov did for codecs like Go To Meeting, represents an order of magnitude more difficulty than a 1 MB blob which itself takes roughly a month of work
evidence: 'one megabyte binary blob to reverse engineer is probably order of magnitude a month of work'
Kostya reverse engineered Go To Meeting codec alone in approximately two months
Process involves disassembly, hooking modules, dumping raw YUV data, and debugging instruction by instruction

claims.claim: The spicy FFmpeg/VideoLAN tweets about corporate exploitation produced positive results including substantially increased donations and greater technical awareness
evidence: VLC Android update was blocked for over a year due to a Play Store bug; a threatening tweet got Android team to respond
VLC has ~100 million users on Android
Donations increased substantially though still not enough for a single full-time developer

claims.claim: Andrew Kelley, creator of the Zig programming language, was an FFmpeg developer before starting Zig
evidence: JB: 'andrew kelly started zig he was an ffmpeg developer and started zig after ffmpeg school'

quote_candidates.text: the problem with security reports in general is security people are rampant self-promoters in parentheses Linus once called them something worse imagine you're a humble volunteer open source developer if a security researcher finds a bug in your code they're going to make up a cute name for it start a website with a logo Google is going to give them a million dollar bounty they're going to go to Defcon and get a prize and I assume there's some kind of secret security people orgy where everyone is dressed like they're in the matrix nobody is going to do any of this for you when you fix it
quote_candidates.speaker: Alex Strange

quote_candidates.text: the xz fiasco has shown how a dependence on unpaid volunteers can cause major problems trillion dollar corporations expect free and urgent support from volunteers
quote_candidates.speaker: Kieran

quote_candidates.text: we are not your supplier if you want me to be a supplier i'm very happy right i would send you a contract
quote_candidates.speaker: JB

quote_candidates.text: if you're good in c in ffmpeg if you know how to write assembly are you sure you you're going to be one of the best programmers ever even if you're working on writing type 3 because this is the most amazing thing to do and you will like have to get reviews by some of the most seasoned programmer ever who are going to look at every part of your code and tell you why it's not great it's like we are the best teachers that you've ever had in programming right
quote_candidates.speaker: JB

quote_candidates.text: He looked at the world as a binary specification. He didn't need documentation or anything It's I have a binary and I can figure all of this out and he regularly used the phrase Binary specification. I know it's not a problem
quote_candidates.speaker: Kieran

quote_candidates.text: many things in the world only happen because an awesome person decides to do it
quote_candidates.speaker: Tim Sweeney

topics: FATE automated testing infrastructure for FFmpeg
Handwritten SIMD assembly optimization vs compiler auto-vectorization
dav1d AV1 software decoder development
Assembly programming pedagogy and community
Rust language debate in multimedia/open-source context

claims.claim: FFmpeg's FATE testing system runs on a massive volunteer-hosted matrix of OS, compiler, and architecture combinations, catching miscompilations that break correctness.
evidence: fate.ffmpeg.org shows dozens of configurations including Mac OS, iOS, tvOS, PowerPC, RISC, DEC Alpha, Visual Studio, Intel compiler, Apple Clang
All systems are volunteer-hosted; the speaker hosts Mac variants in his own office
Even a small miscompilation can cascade into large glitches because video frames have dependencies

claims.claim: Handwritten SIMD assembly in FFmpeg achieves 10x–62x speed improvements over equivalent C code, far exceeding compiler auto-vectorization.
evidence: A specific function shown achieves 62x speedup over C
Speakers state auto-vectorization is 'not even close' and 'multiple times slower', not 5–10% slower
Open Broadcast's internal pixel-format conversion matrix is entirely in handwritten assembly with documented speedups

claims.claim: The dav1d AV1 decoder contains 240,000 lines of handwritten assembly against only 30,000 lines of C, more assembly than all other FFmpeg codecs combined.
evidence: FFmpeg total assembly across all codecs is ~100,000 lines; dav1d alone has 240,000 lines
30% of Netflix traffic and 50% of YouTube traffic is AV1; dav1d runs on ~3 billion devices
With dav1d, 720p decoding is achievable with one or two CPU cores

claims.claim: dav1d deliberately violates standard OS calling conventions in its internal assembly to avoid unnecessary register saves/loads and gain additional speed.
evidence: Standard calling conventions require saving registers when entering a function; dav1d skips this for intra-library calls
At least four calling conventions exist on x86 alone (Linux 32/64, Windows 32/64); dav1d bypasses them internally
This avoids loading and saving registers to L1/L2 cache, improving throughput

claims.claim: VLC supports a wider range of operating systems than Microsoft, Google, or Apple, including Windows XP, macOS 10.7, iOS 9, OS/2, BSD, and Solaris, with a comparatively tiny team.
evidence: The last VLC version still runs on OS/2, which has roughly 10 users worldwide
Supporting iOS 9 requires Frankenstein-mixing multiple Xcode/SDK versions to compile for ARM32
This is possible in part because of FATE and FFmpeg's broad codec/platform support

claims.claim: Rust is unsuitable for rewriting assembly-optimized codecs like dav1d because inline/handwritten assembly destroys Rust's memory-safety guarantees regardless of how the C parts are rewritten.
evidence: Handwritten assembly can jump anywhere in memory, breaking Rust's security model
Speakers argue secure assembly needs compile-time instrumentation checking memory access patterns
JB's startup Kyber uses Rust for new projects but he opposes rewrites of existing assembly-heavy codebases

claims.claim: Reading code is significantly harder than writing it, making rewrites dangerous because developers lose undocumented business logic and edge-case wisdom embedded in existing code.
evidence: JB states it is 'an order of magnitude easier to write code than read code'
LLMs can write code but struggle to analyze existing complex code
JB jokes he cannot understand his own code six months after writing it

quote_candidates.text: Every new codec added is actually worth more than the value of that codec itself because it makes the whole thing better.
quote_candidates.speaker: Kieran O'Leary

quote_candidates.text: We use the machine in ways that the creator didn't expect. Sometimes we use an instruction that's completely unrelated to what we do. We use a cryptography instruction in video processing to do nothing related.
quote_candidates.speaker: Jean-Baptiste Kempf

quote_candidates.text: 30,000 lines of C, but 240,000 lines of handwritten assembly.
quote_candidates.speaker: Jean-Baptiste Kempf

quote_candidates.text: It's like flying a Spitfire. It's really aviation at its purest, but also pushing the aircraft beyond what the designer thought was possible.
quote_candidates.speaker: Kieran O'Leary

quote_candidates.text: It is an order of magnitude easier to write code than read code.
quote_candidates.speaker: Jean-Baptiste Kempf

quote_candidates.text: Even if I rewrite the C part in Rust for security reason, you break all the security when you write handwritten assembly because we can jump anywhere.
quote_candidates.speaker: Jean-Baptiste Kempf

guest_candidates.name: Jean-Baptiste Kempf
guest_candidates.title: Co-founder/President, VideoLAN; Co-founder, Open Broadcast; Founder, Kyber

topics: FFmpeg/LibAV fork and open source governance
Open source maintainer burnout and AI slop
x264 encoder and psychovisual compression
Video codec evolution: H.264 to AV1
VLC security incidents and fake software distribution

claims.claim: The FFmpeg/LibAV split in 2011 was driven by governance and leadership disputes rather than technical disagreements, and LibAV's work was eventually merged back into FFmpeg, making FFmpeg stronger.
evidence: FFmpeg and LibAV split in 2011, mainly over project governance, leadership style, and development processes
FFmpeg effectively absorbed LibAV's work, while LibAV withered and most distributions and developers moved back to FFmpeg
Ubuntu temporarily switched to LibAV around 2012-2014 before switching back

claims.claim: AI-generated low-quality bug reports and patches are increasing maintenance burden on open source developers, contributing to burnout.
evidence: Daniel Steinberg, maintainer of Curl, coined the term 'AI slop' for fake/bad AI-generated reports and patches
The XZ fiasco occurred because one maintainer was hammered by two attackers asking questions nonstop at night until he gave them commit access

claims.claim: x264 revolutionized video encoding by prioritizing psychovisual quality over mathematical metrics like PSNR, which had caused widespread blurring in prior encoders.
evidence: Industry and academia were obsessed with peak signal-to-noise ratio (PSNR/mean-squared error) for 20 years, leading to blurring artifacts
x264 introduced psychovisual rate distortion and adaptive quantization, developed partly through iterative feedback with anime fan communities
Lauren Merrick's stated goal: 'I don't want to test this on a $30,000 screen. I want this to look good on someone's laptop at home'

claims.claim: AV1 achieves 40–60% bandwidth savings compared to H.264 at equivalent visual quality, but requires roughly two orders of magnitude more CPU cycles to encode.
evidence: AV1 saves between 40 and 60% less bandwidth than H.264 for the same quality at a given bit rate
AV1 encoding is two orders of magnitude more CPU-intensive than H.264
YouTube re-encodes only popular videos in AV1 due to cost, while encoding most content in H.264

claims.claim: The CIA used a modified version of VLC with an added DLL to conduct surveillance operations, and Chinese hackers similarly exploited a signed VLC DLL to target Indian users, leading India to temporarily ban VLC.
evidence: CIA Vault 7 documents revealed a custom VLC build with a plugin (PSAPI.DLL) that read the document folder, encrypted it, and sent it out
Chinese hackers used a legitimate signed VLC DLL with a fake libvlc to target Indian users
VLC was banned in India until JB fought in Indian court to get it unbanned

claims.claim: A fake VLC installer has been circulating in Germany for over 12 years, ranks above VideoLAN in German Google results, and deliberately delays malicious activity for three weeks to evade detection.
evidence: Fake German VLC website reported for 12 years with Google failing to act because the binary is too large for their virus analyzer
The fake installer activates a background service only after three weeks to evade detection windows

claims.claim: JB received a death threat with powder included in a letter after deciding to drop PowerPC support for VLC around 2009–2010.
evidence: VLC was approaching version 1.0 with only four developers when the decision was made
The letter included powder reminiscent of Anthrax threats and stated 'PowerPC forever'
JB had to go to the police with his mother over the incident

quote_candidates.text: forks are even, they're important because they change the status quo of a community
quote_candidates.speaker: JB (Jean-Baptiste Kempf)

quote_candidates.text: the XZ fiasco was because there was one guy maintaining it and he got basically hammered by two attackants who were asking him questions nonstop at weird times at night to block him

quote_candidates.text: I don't want to test this on a $30,000 screen. I want this to look good on someone's laptop at home.

quote_candidates.speaker: JB (Jean-Baptiste Kempf)

topics: VLC sandboxing and security architecture
Kyber: ultra-low latency video SDK for robotics and teleoperation
Next-generation codecs: AV2, H.266/VVC, and the patent landscape
Open source licensing and European tech entrepreneurship
Adaptive streaming and multimedia delivery

claims.claim: VLC is being split into multiple sandboxed processes (decoding, de-muxing, filters) to improve security without sacrificing performance, analogous to Chrome's tab isolation.
evidence: VLC has around 500 plugins including FFmpeg, GPU drivers, and third-party codecs running inside the same process
The sandbox must sustain gigabits per second of memory copies, far exceeding typical web application demands
Comparison made to Chrome crashing a tab without crashing the whole browser

claims.claim: Kyber achieves 7 milliseconds glass-to-glass latency currently, with a target of 4 milliseconds, using a single QUIC/UDP socket for multiplexed audio, video, and control streams.
evidence: NVIDIA hardware encoder takes ~3.5ms, Intel decoder ~2ms, totaling ~6ms for codec alone
4 milliseconds corresponds to 240Hz refresh equivalent
Demo at CES showed a 3D-printed rover controlled from France over the internet

claims.claim: AV2 achieves approximately 30% bandwidth reduction over AV1 at equivalent quality, continuing the generational ~30% improvement trend across codec generations.
evidence: HEVC is ~30% better than H.264; H.266 is ~30% better than H.265
AV2 specification is complete and VLC demonstrated first AV2 demo at CES
The AV2 decoder is called DAV2D (David 2), a recursive acronym

claims.claim: HEVC licensing became commercially untenable due to fragmented patent pools, motivating large companies to create the royalty-free AV1/AV2 codecs via the Alliance for Open Media.
evidence: HEVC had multiple patent pools: MPEG-LA, HEVC Advance, Nokia separately outside pools
HP reportedly removed HEVC support from Windows laptops due to increasing patent costs
YouTube/Netflix licensing costs could reach hundreds of millions of dollars per year

claims.claim: Kyber uses forward error correction and clock drift compensation to ensure coherent multi-sensor data synchronization for robot AI training.
evidence: Over-transmitting a few percent of data allows packet loss recovery without adding latency
All robot sensors (cameras, GPS, captors) need synchronized timestamps for valid AI model training
Existing solutions work with one camera but become unreliable with five or more cameras

claims.claim: VLC's build process is intentionally air-gapped and paranoid, compiling on offline machines that bootstrap the compiler, with double signing, after detecting what they believe was a state actor attempting to inject a fake binary.
evidence: Compile boxes start by compiling the compiler and are never connected to the internet
A believed non-Western governmental agency attempted to push a fake binary to VLC's servers
Double signature process is used for release signing

claims.claim: Kyber is dual-licensed under AGPL and a commercial license, making it free for open-source projects but paid for proprietary commercial use.
evidence: If you use Kyber in a product, the full product must be open source under AGPL
Large companies pay for the commercial license to get support, IP rights, and modification rights
Described as same model referenced earlier in the conversation about licenses

quote_candidates.text: The goal of kyber is to make distance disappear because it is a projection of skills or projection of power.
quote_candidates.speaker: Jean-Baptiste Kempf

quote_candidates.text: 4 milliseconds means 240 hertz, right? Yeah, it's nuts.
quote_candidates.speaker: Jean-Baptiste Kempf

quote_candidates.text: There is no code that gets into VLC that we don't control.
quote_candidates.speaker: Jean-Baptiste Kempf

quote_candidates.text: If I had to pay all the license fee for VLC, I needed to pay more than 200 euros per user.
quote_candidates.speaker: Jean-Baptiste Kempf

quote_candidates.text: You shouldn't be able to patent math and matrices. Like this is wrong.
quote_candidates.speaker: Jean-Baptiste Kempf

quote_candidates.text: multimedia is what we call a patent minefield. There is two places where you have the most patents. It's everything related to 3G, 4G, 5G, RF, and multimedia
quote_candidates.speaker: Jean-Baptiste Kempf

topics: VLC privacy and government surveillance resistance
FFmpeg and VLC long-term archival mission
Future of multimedia: spatial audio, XR, brain-computer interfaces
FFmpeg deployment in extreme environments (Mars, CERN, military)
Open source maintenance philosophy

claims.claim: VLC has no telemetry and the international distributed nature of its contributors makes it structurally resistant to government surveillance mandates
evidence: No telemetry in VLC by design
Contributors in UK, Germany, US would all be able to see any surveillance code
French government cannot mandate what content VLC blocks or tracks

claims.claim: FFmpeg is used by the Mars 2020 rover to compress pictures, making it a multi-planetary open source library
evidence: Mars 2020 rover team wrote a paper about wanting to use commercial off-the-shelf technology
FFmpeg was chosen as part of that approach
Kieran Cunha described FFmpeg as 'a multi-planetary open source library'

claims.claim: The archival community, led by Dave Rice from City University of New York, funded development of the FFv1 lossless codec and GPU encoding in FFmpeg specifically for preservation purposes
evidence: FFv1 is a lossless codec designed for fast software-based encoding
GPU encoding in FFmpeg was funded by the archiving community to speed up FFv1 encoding
Archiving community is concerned about lossy compression changing historical material

claims.claim: The BBC's New Doomsday Book, archived on BBC microcomputers, became unplayable within 10-20 years because no one had the right software, illustrating the risk FFmpeg is designed to prevent
evidence: Archive stored on BBC microcomputers
Unplayable within 10-15 or 20 years
Someone had to reverse engineer it to recover the content

claims.claim: FFmpeg and VLC are being expanded toward volumetric video, XR streaming, spatial audio, haptic tracks, and eventually brain-computer interface codecs as multimedia expands to all human senses
evidence: VLC already has a VR and XR version
Kyber streams XR content to low-power glasses including Apple Vision and Quest
VLC has a haptic plugin for 4D cinema systems with hydraulic arms
Spatial audio demo already completed with architecture changes in VLC

claims.claim: VLC is operated with a core legal responsibility held by a single person, Jean-Baptiste Kempf, despite millions of users and military/government interactions
evidence: Six to eight core contributors
Everything legally is only Jean-Baptiste Kempf
US military used VLC on laptops after someone in the military reviewed the open source code

claims.claim: C is the ideal language for thousand-year archival software because it is closest to mathematics and logic, and will endure like Latin
evidence: FFmpeg is written in C
Kieran Cunha argued C will be like Latin: learnable from the past and still usable
Mathematical notation and stable languages persist across centuries

quote_candidates.text: FFmpeg, nothing is beyond our reach.
quote_candidates.speaker: Kieran Cunha

quote_candidates.text: Talk is cheap, send patches.
quote_candidates.speaker: Kieran Cunha

quote_candidates.text: C will be like Latin. It will be a thing that you learn from the past, but it will still be usable in certain contexts.

quote_candidates.text: FFmpeg runs on Mars, so we are a multi-planetary open source library.

quote_candidates.text: Regrets are attacks on your mind. Learn from your mistakes, but don't regret, because you've done it.

quote_candidates.text: In a thousand years, we may be drowning in AI slop. This stuff needs to be important and archived.
